title: media.rs: no GIF decompression bomb limit
description:
LOCATION: src/media.rs:194-214, process_image()

FINDING: The image decode limits only cap max_alloc at 64MB, but GIF is not subject to the same pixel-count arithmetic as JPEG/PNG. An animated GIF with many frames can allocate far more than 64MB when the image crate expands all frames. The 'image' crate's Limits struct only applies to single-frame decoded size; an attacker can send a multi-frame GIF near the 10MB wire limit that expands to hundreds of MB during decode.

IMPACT: OOM or process kill on the server when processing a crafted animated GIF uploaded via the CLI local-file path or a webhook media URL, since both call process_local/process_remote -> store_processed_image -> process_image.
close_reason: Fixed: image::Limits max_image_width/height set in process_image
